course_description:
Introduction to the accounting environment, analysis and interpretation of financial statements and other business information for the purpose of decision making.

This course enables the student to become proficient in how accountants capture, measure and evaluate business decision-making using the techniques of accrual accounting.

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:
restrictions: Not to be studied by BAdvBus(Hons) and BAdvFinEcon(Hons) students

Full name

Course Code
course_aims: This course aims to equip students from a wide variety of backgrounds and disciplines with awareness and knowledge in relevant fields of accounting essential to decision making in business.

learning_outcomes.description: Identify and explain accounting terms and concepts that are relevant to business decision making.
Record and interpret business transactions and financial statements.
Apply relevant and appropriate accounting knowledge and techniques in business problem-solving.
Make and justify effective business operating, financing and investing decisions informed by relevant accounting information.
Demonstrate management and cost accounting techniques, including the use of budgets, to support business decisions.

assessment_details.title: Inspera Case Study Part 1
assessment_details.task_description: Assessment Details:This assessment is based on a case of a retailing business.Assessment materials will be released on Blackboard and discussed in tutorials.Topic assessed: Topics 1-3.Question types: calculations, short answers and recording transactions.Supplementary assessment is not available for this assessment.Inspera cloud-based software is used to complete the assessment.The individual assessment is a non-invigilated digital assessment and students will access require access using their own device. Further details, including an introduction to Inspera functionality, practice opportunities, and where to go for support, will be provided in the Blackboard site for this course.AI StatementArtificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.Read full task descriptionHide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Students are required to submit their assessment via Inspera on Blackboard. Submissions after the deadline will attract penalties.
Deferral or extension
You may be able toapply for an extension.
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
The Inspera Application automatically closes on the due date and time. Therefore, extension requests must be made as soon as possible to ensure the application remains open to enable students the ability to continue working on their assignment. See Blackboard for more details and instructions.
Late submission
Apenaltyof 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Inspera Case Study Part 2
assessment_details.task_description: Assessment details:This assessment is based on a case of a retailing business.Assessment materials will be released on Blackboard and discussed in the Assessment Workshop.Inspera will open for data entry two weeks before the due date.Topic assessed: Topics 1-6.Question types: calculations, short answers and recording transactions.Supplementary assessment is not available for this assignment.Inspera cloud-based software is used to complete the assessment.The individual assessment is a non-invigilated digital assessment and students will access require access using their own device. Further details, including an introduction to Inspera functionality, practice opportunities, and where to go for support, will be provided in the Blackboard site for this course.An Excel file will be provided for students to complete some of the calculation questions before entering the data into Inspera for submission and marking.AI StatementArtificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Assessment Workshop and Drop-in Q&A SessionThe Week 7 lecture is replaced with an assessment workshop held during regular lecture times. This session will review the Inspera Case Study Part 2 requirements and provide students with the opportunity to ask questions. The workshop is informal and student driven.Learning outcomes:L04

Assessment Drop-in Q&AThe Week 8 tutorials are replaced with Assessment Drop-in Consultations held during the regular tutorial times. Students are encouraged to attend to seek help with their Inspera Case Study Part 1 assessment. No formal tutorials will be held.
